mnemonics: HABITS: Histograms Are Bars In The System (to remember that histograms use bars to show frequency distributions)
keyPoints: A histogram visualizes the distribution of a continuous numerical variable.
The x-axis represents the variable's values divided into bins, and the y-axis represents the frequency or count of observations in each bin.
Unlike a bar chart, the bars in a histogram touch each other to emphasize the continuous nature of the data.
commonMistakes: Using histograms for categorical data (a bar chart should be used instead).
Not adjusting the bin width, which can drastically change the interpretation of the distribution (over-smoothing or over-detailing).
